inline_comments.message: nb: suggestion: Can the type be changed to MediaType ?
nb: suggestion: can be a constant. Used at three places

nb: suggestion: 
cmHandle information is more like a set which will be required for other test cases too. Would it be possible to seperate it from the test case. It will make it more readable.
nb: same comment about moving it out of test case
this is inconsistent with convention in other parameter names but I suspect you have doen that to align with the normal 'Accept' header?! Please confirm
Same comment as on 'Accept' above, we might need a quick team decision on these..
should the datasource not be  parameter?!
should this not be a parameter?
3 occurrences is the threshold I think for SonarQube rule  so yes I think it HAs to be extracted now (personally I don't agree with this but dont have an option)

agreed, see https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS-390+Spike%3A+Define+and+Agree+DMI+Plugin+REST+Interface#CPS390Spike:DefineandAgreeDMIPluginRESTInterface-DMIInventory,Model&DataSyncAPI
(never liked 'additional properties' it is to generic)
align all names with agreed name on page for CPS-391
nned to discuss why you did this using hardcode methodenames...
are these test depending on real (registry model) paths?
yes they are standard fields for header and case sensitive, picked them from postman

initially i put it in param but then i needed to do lot of if else in controller
coz 
operation -> get(read), and post put pathch and delete are irrelevant 
passthrough -> get , post , patch , put, delete needed

that why i fixed them in url
passing the string here for any custom content type which is not supported in mediatype
ok, as discussed these are standard header parqmneters and name is defined by that
agreed that this can be addressed later without affect the rest client interface
requestJson variable is not needed, it can be inlined.

Isn't a create error here ?

Suggestion for later improvement: Manipulating http response code should not be bubble up to here, it could be done closer to the rest client call. THis could simplify the code here and also hide the implementation detail about how the integration with dmi is done.
as discussed keeping it for now
as discussed keeping it for now
yes, "/dmi-registry/cm-handles[@id='testCmHandle']" this path is being prepared in ncmp service and it should match in mock.
refactored due to merge issues, moved these private methods down, not related to this commit
is this needed? I thought we only support application/json for now or did we agree to keep it in the openapi but make it default in the service layer?
should this be Ok instead as we agreed to make the create generic?

do we (or SNDC-C) support both options yet (in passthrough)? If not only define the supported option for now or describe what is not supported above...

ANSWER: Need more work in (ONAP) DMI plugin to support this but NCMP itself DOES 'support' it

Rahul and I agreed since this can be ANY format there should be NO enum (NCMP is just a proxy it should not be aware/update for new/proprietary formats)

I sure Dylan and I have a improved version of this which doesn't use 2 separate collections. For some reason I cannot find it now. I will contact Dylan and maybe flag this a little future improvement task...
